topic: Do Student Years Count Toward a Swiss C Permit? How Residence History Is Really Calculated
angle: Explains how student residence, L permits, mixed permit histories, and the final qualifying years affect Swiss C permit eligibility.
notes_for_drafting: Do not imply that all years in Switzerland count in the same way
Separate clearly the first qualifying years from the final residence period
Use mixed-status examples to avoid oversimplifying residence counting

topic: Can You Lose a Swiss C Permit by Staying Abroad Too Long? Absence, Lapse, and Preservation Rules Explained
angle: Shows how long absences abroad can affect C permit validity, when advance preservation may help, and why repeated short returns may not solve the problem.
notes_for_drafting: Do not describe the C permit as unconditional permanent residence
Explain the difference between card renewal and the underlying status
Avoid suggesting that brief return trips automatically preserve settlement

topic: Swiss C Permit Refused After 10 Years? When the Real Problem Is Integration, Debt, or Timing
angle: Helps applicants understand why a canton may extend a B permit instead of granting settlement, and how language, debt, welfare risk, or poor timing may be the real issue.
notes_for_drafting: Do not assume that 10 years of residence makes refusal unusual or irrational
Distinguish timing problems from deeper integration or compliance concerns
Show that refusal may reflect evidence weakness rather than a single legal bar

topic: Time Abroad on a Swiss C Permit: The 6-Month Rule, “Freezing” Your Permit, and Common Traps for Frequent Travellers
angle: Clarify how absences can lead to automatic lapse, how advance authorisation to retain the C permit works, and how to document continued Swiss residence when life is international.
notes_for_drafting: Avoid providing tax residency advice; flag tax implications as separate and fact-specific.
Use clear examples of travel patterns that create risk without suggesting ‘reset’ tricks.
Stress the importance of applying in advance for retention and keeping proof of Swiss centre of life.
Note that cantonal practice and documentation requests can vary.
